title: Icosphere Save Point Animation
description: An interactive save-game UI element built from an icosahedron that expands its faces outward on hover and performs Rubik's-cube-style band rotations along vertex axes. On hover the 20 triangular faces separate with a gap, then groups of faces (caps of 5 or equatorial bands of 10) rotate by 72-degree increments around icosahedral vertex axes. Faces snap back to a perfect grid after each rotation to prevent drift. On mouse-out the whole structure smoothly collapses back into a solid icosphere. A Three.js prototype exists with the core mechanics working: face normalization, axis selection, cap/equator band picking, rotation with snap-to-ideal, and additive-blended emissive materials.
definition-of-done: 1. Icosphere renders in the game engine using SDF or mesh pipeline. 2. On hover, faces expand outward along their normals with a smooth lerp. 3. While expanded, groups of faces rotate around icosahedral vertex axes in 72-degree increments. 4. Rotation bands are correct: caps (5 faces sharing a vertex) and equatorial bands (10 faces). 5. Faces snap to ideal grid positions after each rotation to prevent floating-point drift. 6. On mouse-out, faces smoothly collapse back to a closed icosphere. 7. Visual style: emissive blue-white glow, wireframe overlay, additive blending. 8. Clicking the activated save point triggers the save-game action.
testing-plan: 1. Hover over the icosphere and verify faces expand outward evenly. 2. Watch multiple rotation cycles and verify no faces drift out of alignment. 3. Verify cap rotations move exactly 5 faces and equatorial rotations move ~10 faces. 4. Mouse-out mid-rotation and verify smooth collapse without visual glitches. 5. Rapidly hover on/off and verify no stuck states. 6. Verify the save action fires on click. 7. Test at different camera distances and window sizes.
todos: Port the icosphere face decomposition from the Three.js prototype to the game engine - DONE

Implement icosahedral vertex axis extraction for rotation bands - DONE
Implement cap and equatorial band selection logic - DONE
Implement 72-degree band rotation with quaternion math - DONE
Implement snap-to-ideal after each rotation to fix drift - DONE
Implement smooth collapse back to solid on mouse-out - DONE
Add emissive material, wireframe overlay, and additive blending - IN_PROGRESS
Wire hover detection to the game input system - DONE
Wire click to trigger save-game action - IN_PROGRESS
Add point light at center for inner glow effect - TODO
unknowns: Whether to use the mesh pipeline or SDF for the icosphere faces
How to handle the 3D hover detection (raycasting against individual triangle faces vs bounding sphere)
Whether rotation speed and pause duration need tuning for game feel
Whether additional visual effects are needed (particle trails, glow pulses on snap)
changes-made: Added Python automation CLI scaffolding command `new-scene` with parameterized scene generation, launch.json insertion, and optional Maven profile insertion

Scaffolded and implemented `IcosphereSavePointScene` under `ixdar.scenes.ui` with 3D camera controls, hover detection, expand/collapse animation, randomized 72-degree band rotations, and snap-to-ideal behavior
Added `IcosphereGeometry` data class for icosahedron face decomposition, ideal slot state, axis extraction, and cap/equatorial band selection logic
Added `IcosphereRuntime` for per-face mesh upload/render lifecycle using MeshShader and per-face transforms
Extended mesh fragment shader with emissive/rim glow uniforms used by icosphere rendering
Added launch profile and Maven profile for `icosphere-save-point-canvas` and validated compile + scene startup via Maven profile
Wired primary click activation hook in scene (`Save point activated` log) for save action integration point
